full_response:
Step 1 :  Identify free (“hungry”) vs. dummy (“fed”) indices  
R_{ab}-\frac12\,g_{ab}=G_{ab}

• a appears once in every term.  
• b appears once in every term.  

Because neither index is repeated within a single term, neither is summed over.  
Therefore

 a , b  → free (hungry) indices  
 (no dummy indices are present).

Step 2 :  Rewrite with different index letters  
We may replace every free index by any other symbol, provided we keep the same pair on every term:

R_{μν}-\frac12\,g_{μν}=G_{μν}

(or R_{ij}-½ g_{ij}=G_{ij}, etc.).

Step 3 :  How many separate component equations?  
Each free index can take the four spacetime values 0,1,2,3, so in principle there are 4×4 = 16 equations.  
However R_{ab}, g_{ab}, and G_{ab} are all symmetric (R_{ab}=R_{ba}, g_{ab}=g_{ba}, G_{ab}=G_{ba}), leaving only the 10 independent combinations with a≤b.

Hence the tensor equation is equivalent to 10 independent scalar equations (16 components, 10 independent).

\boxed{\text{Free indices: }a,b\quad\;(\text{no dummy indices})\;;\;\; R_{ab}-\tfrac12 g_{ab}=G_{ab}\;\Longrightarrow\;R_{\mu\nu}-\tfrac12 g_{\mu\nu}=G_{\mu\nu}\;;\;\;10\text{ independent component equations}} 

Confidence: 86%
